name: Archive Implementation

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Archive Implementation is complete and correct

scope: Implements the core auto-archive system as defined by the archive service spec. Covers four concerns: (1) Config types and defaults: add ArchiveConfig to the config tree with auto_archive_delay_hours and auto_archive_enabled fields, wire defaults into base config. (2) State type extensions: add completion timestamp tracking to NodeState/RootIndex, add archived status or flag to index entries so archived nodes are distinguishable from active ones. (3) Core archive service: implement move (relocate node state from active tree to archive storage, update RootIndex), restore (reverse the move, re-integrate into active tree), and delete (permanently remove archived state). (4) CLI commands: wolfcastle archive restore --node <addr>, wolfcastle archive delete --node <addr>, update archive add to also move node state (not just generate markdown). All new and modified code must maintain >90% test coverage.
success_criteria: All three archive operations (move, restore, delete) are implemented and tested with >90% coverage
CLI commands archive add (updated), archive restore, and archive delete are registered and functional
Archive-then-restore round-trip preserves original directory structure and index state
Delete permanently removes archived state but preserves Markdown rollup entries

breadcrumbs.text: Created 3 children: restore-operation, delete-operation, archive-add-state-move. Ordering: restore first (inverse of move, validates round-trip symmetry), delete second (builds on same infrastructure), archive-add-state-move last (refactors shared logic, requires existing tests as safety net). Config types, state extensions, move operation, daemon integration, and status rendering are already implemented on the branch; this plan covers the remaining restore/delete operations and the archive-add upgrade.
